Turn 3: 4E. Incoming 35 damage (16+8+11). Hand: Clothesline(2E, 12+Weak), HB+(2E, 14), Feed+(1E, 10+3 max HP exhaust), TC+(1E, 7 AOE+Vuln), Defend(1E, 5 block). Need block badly vs 35 incoming. Turn 4: Darklings at 13, 32, 23. D1 and D2 defending (12 block each). D0 attacking 7 (Weak). I have Cleave+(1E, 11 AOE) and Reaper+(2E, 5 AOE + heal). Apotheosis(2E, upgrade all). If I play Apotheosis: Cleave already upgraded, Reaper already upgraded, Inflame not upgraded, Defend->8 block, SIO->11 block, Strike->9 dmg. Worth it? Maybe not this turn. Inflame is in draw pile. Let me focus on damage. Cleave+(1E) = 11 AOE. Against D1 (32 HP + 12 block): 11 vs 44 effective = does 0. Against D0 (13 HP): 11 dmg would almost kill (13-11=2 remaining). Against D2 (23 HP + 12 block): 11 vs 35 effective = does 0. Block makes D1/D2 effectively 44 and 35 total. Reaper+(2E) = 5 AOE. Also barely dents through block. The block from Harden is a problem. Need to wait for them to not defend. Plan: Play Apotheosis this turn (free turn with only 7 incoming from D0 Weak). Then next cycle with Inflame I have more Str. Apotheosis(2E) + Defend(1E, 5->wait already 5 unupgraded, Apotheosis upgrades all cards in combat hand and deck so Defend becomes 8) + SIO(1E, 8->11 block + draw). Actually Apotheosis upgrades everything. So after Apotheosis: SIO = 11 block + draw, Defend = 8 block. Apotheosis(2E) + SIO(1E, 11 block + draw) = 3E. Then drawn card might be useful. 1E left for Defend = 8 block. Total block 19 vs 7 incoming = safe. But I am wasting damage opportunity. D0 at 13 HP, not defending. Cleave+(1E) = 11 AOE brings D0 to 2 HP. But I want simultaneous kill. If D0 dies alone, D1 and D2 revive it. Better to not kill D0 alone. Soften D0 and set up for a big Reaper/Cleave kill turn when Str is online. Play Apotheosis(2E) to upgrade all, then SIO(1E) for block+draw, then Defend(1E) for block. Block incoming 7.

Turn 5: 5E. Inflame+ gives +3 Str (upgraded by Apotheosis!). D0 at 13, defending (12 block). D1 at 32 + 12 block = 44 effective, attacking 8. D2 at 23 + 12 block = 35 effective, attacking 11. Incoming 19. Plan: Inflame+(1E, +3 Str). Then with Str 3: Strike+ = 9+3 = 12 dmg. Heavy Blade+ = 14 + 3*3 = 23 dmg. Bash+ = 10+3 = 13 + Vuln. I need to soften these Darklings to similar HP levels for the AOE kill. D0 is at 13 HP (no block next turn since defending now). D1 at 32 (12 block). D2 at 23 (12 block). I cant damage D1/D2 through their block effectively. Heavy Blade+(2E, 23) vs D1 44 effective = does 0 net. Not worth it. Focus on D0? But D0 only has 13 HP, dont want to kill it alone. Actually killing D0 is fine if I can kill D1/D2 soon after? No - Life Link means if D0 dies while others are alive, it revives at half HP (25 HP). That wastes my damage. Better to just block this turn and wait for the Darklings to NOT defend simultaneously. Inflame+(1E) + Defend+(1E, 8 block) + Defend+(... wait I only have 1 Defend+ in hand. Let me think. Hand: Inflame+(1E), Defend+(1E), Strike+(1E), Bash+(2E), HB+(2E). Inflame+(1E) for Str, Defend+(1E) for 8 block, then HB+(2E) on D1 for 23 damage vs 44 effective = 0 net. Ugh. Or just Inflame + block and wait. Inflame(1E) + Defend(1E) = 2E used, 3E left. Strike+ on D0 for 12 damage? D0 at 13 + 12 block = 25 effective. 12 damage = D0 still at 13 HP with 0 block remaining (12 block - 12 dmg = 0 block, 13 HP intact). Good, that removes D0 block without killing it. Then Bash+ on D2 for 13 damage vs 35 effective = D2 block goes 12-13 = -1, so 1 HP damage, D2 at 22 HP. Hmm that barely damages. Total plan: Inflame+(1E) + Strike+ on D0(1E) + Bash+ on D1(2E) + Defend+(1E) = 5E. Against D0: 12 dmg removes 12 block, 0 HP damage. Against D1: 13 dmg vs 12+32=44 effective, removes 12 block + 1 HP damage = D1 at 31 + Vuln 3. D2 untouched. 8 block vs 19 incoming = 11 HP loss. Or I could play HB+ on D1 instead of Bash+. HB+(2E) = 23 damage vs 44 effective D1. Removes 12 block + 11 HP damage = D1 at 21. Then Strike+ on D0 strips D0 block. Inflame+(1E) + HB+(2E) on D1 + Strike+(1E) on D0 + Defend+(1E) = 5E. D1 at 21, D0 block stripped. 8 block vs 19 = 11 HP loss. Better because HB does more damage than Bash through block.

Kill analysis: D0: 1HP+12block=13 effective. D1: 15HP. D2: 23HP+12block=35 effective. Str 3. Cleave+(14) + Reaper+(8) = 22 AOE. Kills D0(22>13) and D1(22>15) but not D2(22<35). Strategy: Do NOT kill D0 alone (it revives). Instead, soften D2 and set up for next turn kill. Play Spot Weakness on D0 (attacking, +4 Str to 7). Then Reaper+ (2E, 12 AOE, heals). Then next turn Cleave should kill all. Str 7: D0 at 1 HP, D1 at 3, D2 at 23 (no block). Cleave = 18 AOE. Kills D0 and D1, D2 at 23-18=5. Still not enough. Need Cleave+Reaper or 2 AOE hits. Actually wait - Spot Weakness+ gives +4 Str. With Str 7: Cleave+ = 11+7 = 18. Need D2 below 18 HP before the kill turn. D2 currently at 23. Reaper does 12 to D2 = strips 12 block, 0 HP damage. D2 still at 23. Hmm. Unless I also play Cleave this turn... Spot Weakness(1E) + Cleave+(1E, 18 AOE) + Reaper+(2E, 12 AOE) = 4E. D0: 1+12block. Cleave 18 vs 13 effective = kills D0. D0 dies. Life Link revives... unless all die. D1: Cleave 18 > 15, kills D1. D2: 12 block. Cleave 18 strips block (12-18=-6, 6 HP damage) = D2 at 17. Then Reaper 12 = D2 at 5. D0 and D1 died from Cleave but D2 alive, so D0/D1 revive. Bad. Play order: If Reaper first, THEN Cleave. Reaper(12 AOE): D0: 12 vs 13 effective = does not kill (0 HP damage, 1 block left). D1: 12 of 15 = D1 at 3. D2: 12 strips 12 block, 0 HP damage. Then Cleave(18 AOE): D0: 18 vs 1 HP + 1 block = 2 effective. Kills D0. D1: 18 vs 3 = kills. D2: 18 vs 23 = D2 at 5. D0 and D1 die, D2 lives, Life Link revives them. ARGH. OK so I cannot kill all 3 this turn. Best play: Spot Weakness(1E, +4 Str) + SIO(1E, block+draw) + SIO(1E, block+draw) + Reaper+(2E, AOE 12 + heal). Total 5E, only have 4E. Drop one SIO. Spot Weakness(1E) + SIO(1E) + Reaper+(2E) = 4E. With Str 7, Reaper does 12 AOE. D0: 12 vs 13 effective = survives (1 block). D1: 12 of 15 = 3 HP. D2: 12 strips 12 block = 0 HP damage, D2 at 23. Then next turn I need to kill all 3 with AOE. D0 at 1 HP (probably no block), D1 at 3 HP, D2 at 23 HP. Cleave at Str 7 = 18 AOE. Kills D0 and D1, D2 at 5. Still need a second AOE or targeted hit on D2. Maybe I should hold Cleave and play 2 AOE next turn. Or better yet: this turn focus targeted damage on D2. Skip Reaper. Play Spot Weakness(1E) + SIO(1E, 11 block + draw) + SIO(1E, 11 block + draw). 3E used. Then remaining cards + draws. 22 block vs 18 incoming. Let me just tank damage and set up optimally. Actually, simplest plan: Spot Weakness on D0(1E, +4 Str to 7), then 2x SIO(2E, 22 block + 2 draws), 1E left. Incoming 10+8=18. 22 block handles it. Then next turn at Str 7, I should have drawn 2 more cards (7 card hand) plus normal draw. Velvet Choker limits to 6 plays though. OK this is getting complicated. Let me just play efficiently.

Then next turn draw 5 from the 5-card draw pile (2x Strike+, Clothesline+, Defend+, Thunderclap+). Thunderclap+ with Str 7 = 7+7=14 AOE + Vuln. Cleave+ in discard (need reshuffle). Hmm. OR: This turn, kill D1 with Headbutt+(1E, 16>15), iron wave D0 to weaken further... D0 already at 1HP. Just kill D0 too. IW+(1E) on D0 + Headbutt+(1E) on D1 + Defend+(1E) = 3E. D0 dead, D1 dead, D2 alive = D0 and D1 revive at half HP. Worse! OK best plan: Soften D2 and block. Bludgeon+(3E) on D2. 39 vs 23 effective = kills D2. D2 dead, D0/D1 alive = D2 revives at half (26 HP). Hmm. Actually: 39 damage. 12 block absorbed. 27 HP damage to D2 (11 HP). D2 dies. D0/D1 alive so D2 revives at 26 HP. Net: replaced D2 23 effective with D2 26 HP next turn. Worse! Killing one makes it worse because revive. Best plan: Chip D2 without killing it, block damage. IW+(1E) on D2 for 14 damage vs 23 effective = strips 12 block + 2 HP damage = D2 at 9 HP. Then Headbutt+(1E) on D2 for 16 damage vs 9 HP = kills D2. But D2 revives. Hmm. No, once 12 block is stripped by IW+, Headbutt does 16 vs 9 HP = D2 dies. But D0/D1 alive = D2 revives. WAIT: What if I kill all within same turn using IW+ then Headbutt sequence? IW on D0 (kills, 1HP), Headbutt on D1 (kills, 16>15), then need to kill D2 same turn. No energy. I keep hitting the same 4E vs 5E problem. Best option: This turn play defensively. Block 21 incoming. IW+ on D2 (1E, strips block + 2 damage = D2 at 9), Defend+(1E, 8 block), Defend+(1E, 8 block) = 3E. 16 block vs 21 = 5 HP loss. D2 at 9 HP. Next turn I draw from shuffled pile: Cleave+, etc. With Str 7, Cleave+ = 18 AOE. D0: 1HP, D1: 3HP (no block if not defending), D2: 9HP. Cleave 18 kills all 3! If D1 defends again (12 block), 18 still > 15. If D2 defends (12 block), 18 < 21. Might need Thunderclap first. Risky but better than wasting kills on Life Link revival.
